ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general grilled beef steak entry; no specific lean ribeye variant exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers grilled beef meat broadly; no lean ribeye-specific code exists. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for lean grilled beef ribeye steak without risk of fabrication.

serving.description: 1 serving (approx. 150 g cooked lean ribeye steak)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of grilled lean beef ribeye steak is approximately 150 g of cooked lean muscle meat, equivalent to a palm-sized portion.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, rib eye steak, lean, grilled — Singapore HPB Foodo (F06030029); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Beef, rib, small end (ribs 10-12), separable lean only, trimmed to 0-inch fat, all grades, cooked, broiled (FDC ID 174033)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for grilled lean beef ribeye steak (F06030029). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174033 used as cross-reference for saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and micronutrient values not reported by HPB.

gi_source.title: N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates; GI not measurable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is not applicable for grilled lean beef ribeye steak as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100g). GI is not measurable for foods with no digestible carbohydrate. The numeric value 0 is used as a sentinel to satisfy the requirement that glycemic_index be numeric; gi_category is set to not_applicable to convey that GI testing is inapplicable. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (49 mg/100g) and favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio (350 mg K : 49 mg Na); lean cut with saturated fat ~1.7 g/100g
hypertension.guidance: Lean grilled ribeye is acceptable for hypertensive individuals when prepared without added salt or high-sodium sauces; limit portion to 100–150 g per meal and pair with potassium-rich vegetables to support blood pressure management per AHA dietary guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero sugar; very high protein (31.9 g/100g) supports satiety and glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Lean grilled ribeye has no glycaemic impact and is an appropriate high-protein food for individuals with type 2 diabetes per ADA Standards of Medical Care; avoid sweet glazes or sugar-containing marinades and pair with non-starchy vegetables for a balanced meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 1.7 g/100g is within acceptable range for a lean cut, but red meat consumption is associated with modest cardiovascular risk; cholesterol 70 mg/100g and trace trans fat (~0.1 g/100g ruminant origin)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lean grilled ribeye may be consumed in moderation (up to 1–2 servings per week) by individuals with cardiovascular disease; choose lean cuts, avoid added saturated fats during cooking, and follow AHA guidance to limit total red meat intake in favour of fish and plant proteins.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat contains arachidonic acid which may amplify inflammatory cytokines; trace ruminant trans fat present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit red meat to 1–2 servings per week per EULAR dietary recommendations and consider replacing with omega-3-rich fish such as salmon or mackerel as the primary protein source.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled beef ribeye steak (lean) is a high-protein, zero-carbohydrate red meat cut sourced from the rib section of beef cattle. The lean preparation involves removal of all separable external and internal visible fat before and after grilling, resulting in a significantly reduced fat content of approximately 4.5 g/100g compared to untrimmed ribeye. The steak is grilled until cooked through, developing a slightly charred exterior through Maillard browning while retaining a tender, juicy interior. The flavour profile is rich, beefy, and savoury with a firm yet succulent texture. Per 100 g, this food provides 169 kcal, 31.9 g protein, 4.5 g fat (of which ~1.7 g is saturated), 0 g carbohydrate, 49 mg sodium, 350 mg potassium, 2.2 mg iron, and 70 mg cholesterol. It is an excellent source of complete protein, haem iron, zinc (5 mg/100g), selenium (28 mcg/100g), vitamin B12 (2.4 mcg/100g), and niacin (8.5 mg/100g). Clinically, it is suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes due to its zero glycaemic impact, and acceptable for hypertension when prepared without added salt. Cardiovascular and rheumatoid arthritis patients should moderate red meat intake. The food is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is widely consumed in Western cuisine as a main course at lunch or dinner.

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (F06030029) with confidence 0.95. Secondary micronutrient values cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for lean grilled beef rib (FDC ID 174033), an approved primary source. All core macros are consistent between sources.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as lean grilled beef ribeye steak is a pure animal muscle protein with no carbohydrate content; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as animal muscle tissue contains no dietary fibre. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo entry and lean muscle beef is not a significant vitamin A source; detailed RAE data not available in the HPB database for this entry. Folate_mcg_dfe set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo entry for this food. Vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo entry; lean beef muscle is not a significant vitamin D source. Vitamin_e_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo entry for this food. Saturated fat 1.7 g/100g, monounsaturated_fat_g 1.9 g/100g, polyunsaturated_fat_g 0.2 g/100g, and trans_fat_g 0.1 g/100g (ruminant origin, naturally occurring) estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174033 as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown. Glycemic index: this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100g); GI testing is not applicable. glycemic_index is set to the numeric sentinel value 0 to satisfy the requirement that the field be numeric, with gi_category set to not_applicable to convey inapplicability. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. Occasion tag 'breakfast' from input retained in meal_context but primary meal context is lunch/dinner for a grilled steak. Ontology codes are closest defensible matches; no exact lean ribeye grilled code exists in FoodOn or FoodEx2 as of 2026.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index is set to numeric value 0 (sentinel for not-applicable, as the food has 0.0 g/100g carbohydrate and GI testing is inapplicable). gi_category set to not_applicable. glycemic_load_per_serving 0.0. gi_source present with full explanation. The validator required glycemic_index to be numeric; 0 is used as the numeric sentinel with gi_category not_applicable to convey inapplicability without using null.
